title: Understanding and access: making sense of government services
description: These free, in-person information sessions are run by Services Australia and the Department of Health, Ageing and Disability. They provide clear, practical information and time to ask questions in a calm, welcoming setting.
address: City Library, 253 Flinders Lane, Melbourne VIC 3000, East Melbourne Library, 122 George Street, East Melbourne VIC 3002, Jean McKendry Neighbourhood Centre, 91-111 Melrose Street, North Melbourne VIC 3051, Kathleen Syme Library, 251 Faraday Street, Carlton VIC 3053, Kensington Neighbourhood Centre, 18 Anthony Street, Kensington VIC 3031, Library at The Dock, 107 Victoria Harbour Promenade, Docklands VIC 3008, narrm ngarrgu Library, 141 Therry Street, Melbourne VIC 3000, North Melbourne Library, 66 Errol Street, North Melbourne VIC 3051, Southbank Library, 207 City Road, Southbank VIC 3006

title: Line dancing
description: Line dancing sessions for all levels at Jean McKendry Neighbourhood Centre.
frequency: Every Wednesday (excluding public holidays), 2pm to 3pm and 3.15pm to 4pm
address: Jean McKendry Neighbourhood Centre, 91-111 Melrose Street, North Melbourne

title: Intermediate tai chi
address: Fawkner Park - meet at South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre, 65 Toorak Road West, South Yarra

title: Sensory Garden
description: A free program to enjoy gardening in our beautiful, tranquil garden.
address: Kensington Neighbourhood Centre, 18 Anthony Street, Kensington

title: Chatty Cafe
description: A friendly, drop-in social event with afternoon tea running weekly on Thursdays. Fortnightly tech help with librarians.
address: Kensington Neighbourhood Centre, 18 Anthony Street, Kensington

title: Beginners tai chi
description: Experience the health benefits of tai chi's slow, gentle and tranquil movements, perfect for beginners.
cost: Free, register to receive link to join with Zoom

title: Connections Program
description: If you are an older person living in the City of Melbourne (or care for someone who is), you can contact the Connections Program Lead.
frequency: Various dates, drop in from 10am to 12pm
address: South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre, 65 Toorak Road West (in Fawkner Park), South Yarra, Kensington Neighbourhood Centre, 18 Anthony Street, Kensington, Jean McKendry Neighbourhood Centre, 91-111 Melrose Street, North Melbourne, East Melbourne Library, 122 George Street, East Melbourne, Library at the Dock, 107 Victoria Harbour Promenade, Docklands, narrm ngarrgu Library and Family Services, 141 Therry Street, Melbourne, North Melbourne Library, 66 Errol Street, North Melbourne, Kathleen Syme Library and Community Centre, 251 Faraday Street, Carlton

title: Monthly carers' walk
description: Connect with other carers during this walking group and morning tea especially for carers.
frequency: First Monday of the month (excluding public holidays)
address: South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre, 65 Toorak Road West, South Yarra

title: Stronger together
description: Increase strength, improve balance, coordination and mobility.
frequency: Every Monday, 1pm to 2pm AND 2.15pm to 3.15pm (excluding public holidays)
address: Kensington Neighbourhood Centre, 18 Anthony Street, Kensington

title: Cards and dice
description: Board games and activities at South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre.
address: South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre, 65 Toorak Road West, South Yarra

title: Meditative movement
description: Refresh your body, mind and heart with gentle yoga poses and guided relaxation.
cost: Free, register to receive link to join with Zoom or to attend in person
address: Online via Zoom, OR, South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre, 65 Toorak Road West, South Yarra

title: Creative Wednesdays
description: Art and craft session for all skill levels. No experience necessary.
frequency: Every fortnight on Wednesday (excluding public holidays), 10am to midday
address: South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre, 65 Toorak Road West, South Yarra

title: Tech help at Kensington Chatty Cafe
description: Drop in to improve your digital skills and get help with your mobile phone and computer with librarians.
frequency: Fortnightly on Thursday (excluding public holidays), 1pm to 3pm
address: Kensington Neighbourhood Centre, 18 Anthony Street, Kensington

title: Afternoon Social
description: Join this free monthly afternoon tea for social connection and lots of fun.
frequency: Second Friday of the month (excluding public holidays), 2pm to 3.30pm
address: South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre, 65 Toorak Road West, South Yarra

title: Books and biscuits
description: Join our librarians every fortnight to read together and talk books while enjoying tea and biscuits.
frequency: Wednesday fortnights (excluding public holidays), 11am to midday
address: South Yarra Senior Citizens Centre, 65 Toorak Road West, South Yarra

title: Kensington Carers Lunch and Mingle
description: Enjoy great food and connect with other local carers.
address: Kensington Neighbourhood Centre, 18 Anthony Street, Kensington
